summary: Iteration 4 backend + frontend smoke complete. 20 new tests in /app/backend/tests/test_iteration4.py all PASS, and the 34 iteration-3 regression tests (backend_test.py + test_perf_cache_rate.py) also all PASS — total 54/54. Verified: (1) PATCH /api/auth/users/{id} admin-only role/status updates with providers-cache invalidation, audit row, 400 on empty body / self-demote / self-disable, 403 for non-admin, 404 for unknown user, 422 for extra fields. (2) /api/notifications masked branch writes a notifications:list:* Redis key on first call (cache MISS), second call is a HIT that increments ccms_cache_hits_total; a new booking invalidates the prefix via the subscriber; unmask=true returns live cleartext, writes a notification.unmasked audit row, and produces ZERO new notifications:* cache keys — the critical 'unmask never populates cache' invariant is upheld. (3) /api/perf/connection-info returns write{topology_type=Single, nodes=['localhost:27017'], read_preference='Primary()'} and read{... read_preference starts with 'SecondaryPreferred('} and same_client=false; non-admin 403. (4) /api/metrics is public, content-type 'text/plain; version=0.0.4', exposes all required metric names (ccms_cache_hits/misses/sets/invalidations/errors_total, ccms_db_queries_total with route labels write/read/read_after_write, ccms_redis_up, ccms_rate_limit_blocks_total, ccms_http_request_duration_seconds_bucket); traffic increases hits + read counters; histogram has positive buckets on /api/patients and path_prefix does NOT explode with dynamic UUIDs; ccms_redis_up flips 1→0 after `supervisorctl stop redis` and back to 1 after start. HIPAA regressions all green (encryption-at-rest enc:v1:, masking by default, break-glass reason, MFA flow, step-up reauth, 7-year soft-delete, 6 mock notifications per appointment lifecycle, /api/perf/stats unchanged, login rate-limit 429 on the 31st attempt). Frontend smoke: admin login → Dashboard / Patients / Appointments / Calendar / Notifications / Security / Audit Log — all pages render, zero console errors, masked PHI ([redacted]) displayed correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: services/identity/router.py::update_user correctly invalidates PREFIX_PROVIDERS on any PATCH, but it ALSO invalidates on pure status='active'→'active' no-op (since the field is present in the payload). Not a bug, just a micro-waste — low priority.

services/perf/router.py::connection_info uses `client.read_preference` which returns the repr of the pymongo ReadPreference. The startswith('Primary') and 'Secondary' substring checks work but a future pymongo version could change that string. Consider exposing `mode` as a separate stable field if this contract is relied on by tests long-term.

services/communication/router.py: the unmask branch does NOT call `await cache.invalidate_prefix(PREFIX_NOTIFICATIONS)` — which is correct (unmask must be cacheless and preserve the masked cache) but worth an inline comment to prevent a future refactor from 'optimising' it.

core/metrics.py: ccms_redis_up is refreshed inside `render()` which makes the gauge consistent per-scrape but will briefly report 1 if Redis drops between two scrapes. Acceptable trade-off; already behaves correctly in the stop-redis test.
core/metrics.py Histogram labels `path_prefix` — good UUID-stripping observed in test_path_prefix_does_not_explode_with_ids. The strip logic correctly groups /api/patients/{uuid} under /api/patients.
Iteration-3 review items still applicable (docstring fix 'fixed-window', read_ratio drift over time) but they are non-blocking operator hints, not bugs.
seed_data_creation: Iteration 4 test run created TEST_it4_*@ccms.app user rows (mix of staff→doctor promotions, disabled/active flips) and a few far-future (2099-2150) appointments used to trigger notification subscribers. All prefixed TEST_it4_ for easy cleanup; safe to leave in place.

redis_verified.providers_cache_invalidated_on_patch: YES — identity:providers:active EXISTS==0 after PATCH role or status
redis_verified.unmask_never_cached: VERIFIED — notifications:* keyspace delta == 0 after GET /api/notifications?unmask=true
redis_verified.subscriber_invalidation_on_booking: VERIFIED — prior notifications:* keys cleared after POST /api/appointments
redis_verified.gauge_flip_on_stop: VERIFIED — ccms_redis_up flipped 1→0 within 2s of `supervisorctl stop redis`, restored to 1 after start
redis_verified.left_running: YES — redis brought back up at end of run

metrics_verified.traffic_increases_counters: ccms_cache_hits_total and ccms_db_queries_total{route=read} both monotonic increase after /api/patients traffic
metrics_verified.no_uuid_path_explosion: VERIFIED — no dynamic-id path_prefix labels found

hipaa_regressions: NONE — all 24 iteration-2 tests + 10 iteration-3 tests pass (encryption enc:v1:, masking, break-glass reason, MFA, step-up reauth, soft-delete, 6 notifications per lifecycle, perf/stats shape, login rate-limit 429 on 31st attempt).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend suite is now 3 files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (HIPAA, 24), /app/backend/tests/test_perf_cache_rate.py (perf, 10), /app/backend/tests/test_iteration4.py (iter4, 20). Run everything with `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://certify-platform-2.preview.emergentagent.com pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`. test_iteration4.py::TestMetrics::test_redis_up_gauge_flips_when_stopped temporarily stops supervisord redis (~3s) and restores it; if a prior run is killed mid-test, run `sudo supervisorctl start redis`. The booking helper in TestNotificationsCache uses randomised far-future timestamps (2099-2150) to avoid conflicts across runs. The rate-limit test remains IP-scoped, can consume login bucket ~60s — schedule rate-limit-sensitive tests last or use X-Forwarded-For.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no bugs found in iteration 4. Previous iteration-3 bug (POST /api/auth/users missing providers-cache invalidation) is no longer reproducible — create_user now calls cache.invalidate_prefix(PREFIX_PROVIDERS). PATCH /api/auth/users/{id} also does it. Providers cache now invalidates correctly on all 4 user-mutation paths (create, patch, disable, enable).
